title: A water activated retrieval device and an object having the same
abstract: The invention relates to a water activated retrieval device for retrieval of an object in case of submersion into water. The device comprises a base module attachable to the object, and a floating module releasably coupled to the base module via a coupling mechanism. The device further comprises a water soluble tablet associated with the coupling mechanism such that the tablet in a dry circumstances locks the coupling mechanism in a coupled state and in wet circumstances releases the coupling mechanism towards a decoupled state.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A water activated retrieval device for retrieval of an object in case of submersion into water, the device comprising a base module attachable to the object, a floating module releasably coupled to the base module and a coupling mechanism coupling the base module to the floating module, wherein the device further comprises a water soluble tablet associated with the coupling mechanism such that the tablet in a dry circumstances locks the coupling mechanism in a coupled state and in wet circumstances releases the coupling mechanism towards a decoupled state. 2. A water activated retrieval device according to claim 1, wherein the water soluble tablet is arranged to dissolve in the wet circumstances. 3. A water activated retrieval device according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the water soluble tablet contains salt particles and/or forms a salt tablet. 4. A water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the coupling mechanism includes a movable element that is kept in a locking position by the water soluble tablet, in the coupled state. 5. A water activated retrieval device according to claim 4, wherein the movable element is arranged to induce a coupling force, in the locking position, and to weaken the coupling force when moving from said locking position. 6. A water activated retrieval device according to claim 4 or 5, wherein the coupling mechanism includes a biasing unit for biasing the movable element away from the locking position. 7. A water activated retrieval device according to claim 4, 5 or 6, wherein the coupling mechanism includes a first coupling module having the movable element and being associated with either the base module or the floating module, and wherein the coupling mechanism further includes a 

second coupling module associated with the other of the base module or the floating module, respectively, and coupled to the first coupling module, in the coupled state. 8. A water activated retrieval device according to claim 7, wherein the second coupling module includes a coupling structure, and wherein the movable element of the first coupling module is arranged to induce a clamping force on the coupling structure of the second coupling module, in the coupled state. 9. A water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims 4 to 8, wherein the movable element of the first coupling module is an arm or lever. 10. A water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the base module and the floating module, in the coupled state, define a cavity receiving the water soluble tablet and allowing water to enter the cavity in case the device has submerged into water. 11. A water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising a retrieval line interconnecting the base module and the floating module. 12. A water activated retrieval device according to claim 11, further including a spool associated with either the base module or the floating module, wherein the retrieval line is wound up on the spool, in the coupled state. 13. A water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the floating body includes a floating element. 14. A water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising a biasing element biasing the base module and the floating module away from each other. 15. A water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the floating body is provided with a mass element, at a side thereof. 16. A water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the floating body is provided with a light emitting element, preferably at a side of the floating body opposite to the side where a mass element is provided. 17. A water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the tablet is provided with a hole or recess. 18. A water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the water soluble tablet and the coupling mechanism are integrated in a force-closed or form-fitting manner. 19. A water activated retrieval device according to claim 12, wherein the spool is bearing-mounted. 20. An object provided with a water activated retrieval device according to any of the preceding claims 1-19 for retrieval of said object in case of submersion into water. 21. An object according to claim 20, comprising a mobile electronic communication device. 22. An object according to claim 20 or 21, wherein the base module of the water activated retrieval device is attached to a backside of the object. 23. An object according to any of claims 20-22, wherein the base module of the water activated retrieval device is integrated with a housing or a cover of the object.
